sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Aston Villa have shown strong form in their last 5 matches, securing 4 wins, including impressive away victories against Chelsea and West Ham. Nottingham Forest, however, are struggling, with 3 consecutive losses in their recent 5 games, highlighting defensive vulnerabilities. Villa's attacking consistency gives them a clear edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, both teams have secured a win each, with Aston Villa winning 2-1 at home and Nottingham Forest taking a dramatic 2-1 victory at home with late goals. Matches between these sides often see both teams scoring, with 6 of the last 8 games featuring goals from both ends.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Aston Villa, sitting 3rd with 39 points, boast a potent attack with 30 goals scored this season, though they underperform their expected goals. Nottingham Forest, languishing at 17th with 18 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 30 goals, and lack firepower with only 18 goals scored.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Villa's Morgan Rogers has been in scintillating form, netting 4 goals in his last 5 matches, while Ollie Watkins remains a constant threat. Forest lack standout performers in recent games, with key midfielder Ibrahim Sangaré missing, further weakening their midfield creativity.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Villa's ability to create big chances (40 this season) and their high possession game (51.6%) could dominate Forest, who struggle with ground duels (48.5% won). Forest’s reliance on late goals might not suffice against Villa’s structured defense despite recent leaks.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Villa's superior form, league position, and attacking prowess, coupled with Forest's recent struggles, a home win seems likely, aligning with Sofascore's 66% probability. Expect Villa to control the game and exploit Forest's defensive frailties, though both teams scoring remains a possibility based on historical trends.
